channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Polls open in Colombia’s pivotal presidential election
item.description: Colombians headed to the polls to choose a new president in a high-stakes election.

item.title: Dozens killed in explosives depot blast in northeast Myanmar
item.description: At least 46 people have been killed and 70 wounded in explosion in rebel-held area, local media report.

item.title: The Bazaar of Return in Aida Refugee Camp
item.description: The Bazaar of Return in Aida Refugee Camp

item.title: Ethiopia’s election: Parties, coalitions and candidates explained
item.description: Over 50 million Ethiopians are registered to vote, with youth and women forming a significant share of the electorate.

item.title: Iran ⁠restores some gas production at South Pars facility
item.description: Production restored at three offshore platforms after Israeli attacks, Pars Oil and Gas Company CEO tells Iranian media.

item.title: Colombians head to the polls to choose President Gustavo Petro’s successor
item.description: The contest is largely a three-horse race between a left-wing senator, a businessman and a right-wing lawmaker.

item.title: Juan Manuel Santos on Colombia’s peace process, 10 years on
item.description: The former Colombian president reflects on peace, conflict and the return of violence.

item.title: Germany World Cup 2026 team preview: Players to watch, group and squad list
item.description: The 2014 champs exited in the group stages of the last two World Cups but look a stronger force under Julian Nagelsmann.

item.title: Rescuers race to save two people still trapped in cave in Laos
item.description: Rescuers face heavy rains, equipment failures in search for two people trapped in central Laos cave by flash floods.

item.title: Could Democrats be shut out of the California governor’s race?
item.description: California is a Democrat stronghold, but thanks to the state's jungle primary, Republicans are frontrunners to advance.

item.title: Iran reinstates some internet access but restrictions remain for most
item.description: Not all data centres are back online while internet protocols remain blocked, restricted or 'whitelisted'.

item.title: What are the chances for the Iran-US ‘Declaration of Principles’?
item.description: Analysts Negar Mortazavi and Colin Clarke dissect the complications surrounding the Iran-US deal.

item.title: Not just the US: How 51 countries armed Israel during Gaza war
item.description: An Al Jazeera investigation has found that military-related goods from at least 51 countries and territories continued e

item.title: Israeli airstrikes devastate Lebanon’s Tyre
item.description: Video from southern Lebanon shows widespread destruction after Israel repeatedly bombed the city of Tyre.

item.title: World Health Organization hails recovery of five Ebola patients
item.description: More than 220 people are suspected to have died from the disease's latest outbreak, which was declared two weeks ago.

item.title: Protesters want to send ‘fascists to Mars’ aboard mock rocket
item.description: Environmental activists in Rome built a mock rocket with caricatures of Giorgia Meloni and Donald Trump.

item.title: Israeli forces push past Lebanon’s Litani River: How significant is it?
item.description: Analysts say Israel is effectively expanding its area of military control northward towards the Zahrani River.

item.title: Satellite imagery shows erasure of southern Gaza as Israel expands control
item.description: Satellite images show transformation as Netanyahu orders military to occupy 70 percent of Gaza.

item.title: Japan rejects ‘new militarism’, says China is rapidly arming
item.description: Japan accuses China of lacking military transparency and stresses the importance of dialogue for regional stability.

item.title: South Africa’s World Cup 2026 departure delayed over Mexican visa debacle
item.description: World Cup delegation held up as players await visas for Mexico, whom South Africa face in the opening game in 11 days.

item.title: Four-time champion Swiatek out as French Open continues to deliver shocks
item.description: Swiatek handed straight-sets defeat by Ukraine's Marta Kostyuk in the round of 16 a day after Gauff's ouster.

item.title: Dublin protesters demand justice over death of Congolese man
item.description: Dublin protesters demand justice over death of Congolese man

item.title: Rio’s Forever War
item.description: In 2025, Brazil’s deadliest police raid killed 120 people and exposed a pattern of mishandled evidence and impunity.

item.title: Israeli forces cross Lebanon’s Litani River and seize 12th century castle
item.description: Invading Israeli forces have crossed north of Lebanon's Litani River and seized the medieval Beaufort Castle.

item.title: World Cup 2026: How Morocco became a football powerhouse
item.description: Morocco's success is built on an effective strategy, but there has been some upheaval in the run-up to FIFA World Cup.
